t: AI Coding Tools, Agents & LLM APIs for Engineers
d: AI coding deep dives — Copilot vs Cursor vs Claude Code, prompt engineering, AI agents, building with LLM APIs, tool use & MCP, and AI in interviews.

t: AI-Assisted Coding: Best Practices & Pitfalls
d: Use AI coding tools without losing control of your codebase: review every diff, keep real understanding, manage secrets, and avoid technical debt.

t: Building with the Claude & OpenAI APIs
d: Build with the Claude and OpenAI APIs: the Messages API, streaming, prompt caching, token costs, latency, structured output, and security best practices.

t: Copilot vs Cursor vs Claude Code Compared
d: GitHub Copilot, Cursor, and Claude Code compared — how each AI coding tool works, their strengths and weaknesses, and when to use which.

t: Harness Engineering — Building Agents Around LLMs
d: Harness engineering is the scaffolding around an LLM that turns a text model into a capable agent: the agent loop, context, tools, memory, and guardrails.

t: How AI Coding Agents Work
d: How AI coding agents work: the agentic loop, tool use, the ReAct pattern, context management, task planning, and how agents self-verify their own output.

t: Prompt Engineering for Code
d: Prompt engineering for code: why prompt quality decides code quality — context, constraints, few-shot examples, planning-first, and test-driven prompting.

t: SDE Interviews in the AI Era
d: When AI can write boilerplate, what do SDE interviews actually test? The durable skills that matter more: system design, debugging, code review, and AI fluency.

t: Tool Use, Function Calling & MCP
d: Function calling, tool use, and the Model Context Protocol (MCP): how models call tools, define JSON schemas, run them in parallel, and why MCP matters.

t: Using AI in Coding Interviews
d: When can you use AI in a coding interview? Where AI tools are banned versus allowed, how to prepare smarter with AI, and how to discuss it with interviewers.

t: Contact & Mock Interview
d: Book a 1:1 mock interview or consultation for SDE interviews. System design and coding interview prep.

t: DDIA Ch.1 — Reliable, Scalable & Maintainable
d: Reliability, scalability, and maintainability — the three pillars of data systems: fault tolerance, load parameters, percentile latency, and operability.

t: DDIA Ch.10 — Batch Processing
d: Batch processing with the Unix philosophy, MapReduce and distributed filesystems, joins and grouping, dataflow engines like Spark and Flink, and batch outputs.

t: DDIA Ch.11 — Stream Processing
d: Stream processing: event streams, message brokers, log-based vs traditional queues, change data capture, event sourcing, time and windows, and stream joins.

t: DDIA Ch.12 — The Future of Data Systems
d: The future of data systems: data integration via derived data, unbundling databases, dataflow and materialized views, end-to-end correctness, and data ethics.

t: DDIA Ch.2 — Data Models & Query Languages
d: Relational vs document vs graph data models, impedance mismatch, schema-on-read vs schema-on-write, and declarative vs imperative query languages.

t: DDIA Ch.3 — Storage & Retrieval
d: How databases store and retrieve data: log-structured storage, SSTables, LSM-trees, B-trees, secondary indexes, column stores, and OLTP vs OLAP.

t: DDIA Ch.4 — Encoding & Evolution
d: Data encoding for storage and transmission, backward vs forward compatibility, schema evolution with Protobuf/Thrift/Avro, and the three modes of dataflow.

t: DDIA Ch.5 — Replication
d: Replication strategies: single-leader, multi-leader, and leaderless; sync vs async; failover dangers; replication lag anomalies; and quorum reads/writes.

t: DDIA Ch.6 — Partitioning
d: Partitioning and sharding: key-range vs hash partitioning, hot spots and skew, secondary indexes by document vs term, rebalancing, and request routing.

t: DDIA Ch.7 — Transactions
d: Transactions and ACID, weak isolation levels (read committed, snapshot isolation/MVCC), lost updates, write skew and phantoms, and serializability via 2PL/SSI.

t: DDIA Ch.8 — The Trouble with Distributed Systems
d: The trouble with distributed systems: partial failure, unreliable networks and clocks, process pauses, fencing tokens, Byzantine faults, and system models.

t: DDIA Ch.9 — Consistency & Consensus
d: Consistency and consensus: linearizability, the CAP theorem, causality and ordering, Lamport timestamps, total order broadcast, two-phase commit, and ZooKeeper.

t: DDIA Notes: Designing Data-Intensive Applications
d: Condensed notes from Designing Data-Intensive Applications (DDIA) — reliability, data models, storage engines, and more.

t: sdeoffer — System Design, Tech Stacks & SDE Interview Prep
d: sdeoffer — software interview prep. System design, tech stacks, and DDIA notes for SDE interviews.

t: Design Google Drive / Dropbox — System Design
d: Google Drive design — chunk files, dedupe by content hash, split metadata from block storage, and sync with change feeds and conflict resolution.

t: Design Nearby / Yelp (Proximity Service)
d: Proximity service design (Yelp) — geohash and quadtree indexing, the cell-boundary problem, read-heavy caching, result ranking, and sharding by region.

t: Design Pastebin — System Design
d: Pastebin system design — generate short keys, separate metadata from blob storage, cache reads behind a CDN, expire old pastes, and block abuse at scale.

t: Design Search Autocomplete (Typeahead)
d: Search autocomplete design (typeahead) — trie-backed prefix lookups, precomputed top-k suggestions, ranking, an offline pipeline, sharding, and caching.

t: Design Ticketmaster — System Design
d: Ticketmaster system design — prevent double-booking with seat holds and locks, expire holds, absorb onsale spikes via a waiting room, and keep inventory strict.

t: Design a Chat App (WhatsApp) — System Design
d: Chat app system design (WhatsApp) — manage millions of WebSocket connections, order messages with Snowflake IDs, deliver offline, and fan out group sends.

t: Design a Distributed Key-Value Store
d: Distributed key-value store design (Dynamo) — consistent hashing, quorum replication, tunable consistency, vector clocks, hinted handoff, and gossip.

t: Design a Hotel Booking System
d: Hotel booking at scale — track room availability, stop oversell with Redis TTL holds, persist bookings as ACID records, and search rooms via Elasticsearch.

t: Design a Leaderboard — System Design
d: Real-time leaderboard design — why SQL ORDER BY stalls, Redis sorted sets for O(log n) rank, durable persistence, sharding, and approximate global rank.

t: Design a Map Web App (Google Maps)
d: Google Maps system design — geospatial indexing, precomputed routing, turn-by-turn navigation over WebSockets, and ML-refined ETAs for a map app at scale.

t: Design a Payment System — System Design
d: Payment system design — route charges through a PSP, block double charges with idempotency, keep a double-entry ledger, reconcile, run sagas, and meet PCI.

t: Design a Ride-Sharing App (Uber) — System Design
d: Uber ride-sharing design — ingest driver GPS at scale, match riders with bipartite graph matching over a geospatial index, and settle fares asynchronously.

t: Design a URL Shortener (TinyURL) — System Design
d: URL shortener design (TinyURL) — encode short keys, weigh hashing vs ID generation, catch collisions with a Bloom filter, purge by TTL, and rate-limit writes.

t: Design Video Streaming Platform (Netflix/YouTube)
d: Video streaming platform design (Netflix, YouTube) — upload and transcode pipelines, adaptive CDN delivery, and ML-powered recommendations at massive scale.

t: Design an E-commerce Platform — System Design
d: E-commerce platform architecture — decompose into microservices, keep inventory consistent, power product search, and process orders with event-driven sagas.

t: System Design Interview Questions & Breakdowns
d: System design interview prep — e-commerce, video streaming, chat app, ride-share, URL shortener and more.

t: API Gateway & Service Mesh
d: API gateways and service meshes handle microservice cross-cutting concerns: north-south/east-west traffic, the BFF pattern, sidecars, mTLS, and gateway vs mesh.

t: Apache Kafka — Pub/Sub, Partitions & Exactly-Once
d: Apache Kafka as a distributed log: the pub/sub model, producers and consumers, partitions and replicas, acks, exactly-once delivery, and the role of ZooKeeper.

t: CDN — Content Delivery Networks
d: CDNs cut latency and offload the origin with edge caching: PoPs and anycast, cache-control and TTLs, push vs pull, invalidation, and dynamic acceleration.

t: CI/CD — Continuous Integration & Delivery
d: CI/CD pipelines end to end: integration vs delivery vs deployment, build-once immutable artifacts, rolling, blue-green and canary deploys, and rollback.

t: Caching — Cache-Aside & Redis vs Memcached
d: Caching patterns for backend systems: cache-aside, write-through, eviction policies, Redis vs Memcached, and how to avoid cache stampede and inconsistency.

t: DNS — The Internet's Name System
d: DNS resolves names to IPs via recursive and authoritative servers: the hierarchy, record types, caching and TTL, GeoDNS and anycast, and reliability/security.

t: Database Indexing — B-Tree vs LSM-Tree
d: Database indexing from the ground up: B-tree vs LSM-tree, clustered vs secondary, composite and covering indexes, leftmost-prefix, and when not to index.

t: Docker & Containers
d: Docker and containers solve the dependency problem: namespaces and cgroups for isolation, containers vs VMs, image layers, the Dockerfile, registries, and more.

t: Elasticsearch — Search & Analytics Engine
d: Elasticsearch internals: the inverted index, analyzers and tokenization, BM25 relevance, shards and replicas, scatter-gather queries, and queries vs filters.

t: GraphQL vs REST — Schema, Resolvers & When to Use
d: GraphQL vs REST compared for API design: resource modeling and resolvers, the typed schema, request and response tradeoffs, and when to choose each approach.

t: Kubernetes — Pods, Services & Architecture
d: Kubernetes core concepts: pods, services and ingress, deployments and StatefulSets, the master/worker cluster architecture, namespaces, and packaging with Helm.

t: Load Balancing — Algorithms, Layer 4 vs 7
d: Load balancing for scalable systems: balancing algorithms, layer 4 vs layer 7, health checks, sticky sessions, and the tradeoffs that come up in interviews.

t: Message Queue — Delivery Semantics & DLQ
d: Message queues decouple services: why they exist, delivery semantics, pull vs push consumption, dead-letter queues, and how queues compare to Apache Kafka.

t: NoSQL — Document, Key-Value & CAP Theorem
d: NoSQL databases by data model: document, key-value, column-family, and graph stores, the CAP theorem tradeoffs, and when to choose NoSQL over SQL.

t: OAuth 2.0 — Authorization Code & Grant Types
d: OAuth 2.0 grant types and protocol flow: the roles, access tokens, the authorization handshake, and why OAuth handles authorization, not authentication.

t: Object Storage & S3
d: Object storage and S3: object vs block vs file, the flat namespace, multipart/presigned uploads, durability via erasure coding, consistency, and storage tiers.

t: Observability — Metrics, Logs & Traces
d: Observability vs monitoring: the three pillars (metrics, logs, traces), OpenTelemetry, distributed tracing, SLI/SLO/SLA, and alerting on symptoms.

t: Redis — In-Memory Data Structure Store
d: Redis, the in-memory data structure store: why it's single-threaded and fast, data types, RDB/AOF persistence, eviction, replication and Cluster, and use cases.

t: Sharding — Consistent Hashing & Shard Keys
d: Database sharding to scale writes: horizontal vs vertical partitioning, sharding strategies, consistent hashing, hotspot avoidance, and cross-shard queries.

t: Spark Join Strategy — Broadcast & Sort-Merge
d: Spark join strategies and how to pick one: broadcast, shuffle hash, sort-merge and cartesian joins, bucketing to skip shuffles, and when each strategy wins.

t: Tech Stacks Deep Dives — Kafka, Kubernetes & More
d: Tech stacks deep dives — Kafka, Kubernetes, load balancing, caching, sharding, NoSQL, OAuth, Spark, GraphQL vs REST.

t: Vector Databases — Embeddings & ANN Search
d: Vector databases for semantic search: embeddings and similarity metrics, approximate nearest neighbor (HNSW, IVF), RAG retrieval, and hybrid search.

t: WebSockets & Realtime
d: WebSockets for real-time apps: why HTTP falls short, polling vs long-polling vs SSE vs WebSockets, the upgrade handshake, and scaling with a pub/sub backplane.

t: gRPC & Protobuf — HTTP/2 RPC, Streaming & Codegen
d: gRPC and Protocol Buffers explained: contract-first RPC, the protobuf format, HTTP/2, the four streaming call types, codegen, and gRPC vs REST vs GraphQL.

t: sdeoffer — 系统设计、技术栈与 SDE 面试准备
d: sdeoffer — 软件面试准备。面向 SDE 面试的系统设计、技术栈与 DDIA 笔记。

t: AI 编程 — 工具、Agent 与 LLM API
d: AI 编程深入——Copilot vs Cursor vs Claude Code、prompt engineering、AI agent、用 LLM API 构建、tool use 与 MCP,以及面试中的 AI。

t: AI 辅助编程:最佳实践与陷阱 — AI 编程
d: AI 辅助编码的最佳实践与陷阱:审查每份 diff、维持对代码的理解、用 AI 写测试并自我验证、上下文管理、secret/PII 合规、识别 AI 技术债,以及诚实度量生产力。

t: 在编码面试中用 AI — AI 编程
d: 编码面试到底能不能用 AI?三类面试形式、公司政策的允许与禁止、被允许时如何正确使用、如何用 AI 高效备考,以及与面试官坦诚沟通的诚信线。

t: 用 Claude 与 OpenAI API 构建应用 — AI 编程
d: 用 Claude 与 OpenAI API 构建生产应用:Messages API、streaming、prompt caching、token 成本、延迟优化、structured output、密钥安全,以及如何选对模型。

t: Copilot vs Cursor vs Claude Code — AI 编程
d: GitHub Copilot、Cursor 与 Claude Code 对比:从补全到 agent 的自主性谱系、各自如何读取上下文与工作、正面对比表,以及如何按任务大小选对工具。

t: Harness 工程 — AI 编程
d: Harness 工程:把文本模型变成能干 agent 的脚手架——智能体循环、上下文组装、工具设计、记忆、护栏与沙箱、验证与评估,以及为什么能力 ≈ 模型 × harness。

t: AI coding agent 是如何工作的 — AI 编程
d: AI coding agent 的内部机制:agentic loop(plan/act/observe)、tool use 原语、ReAct 推理、上下文管理与压缩、任务规划与 sub-agent、自我验证、失败模式与护栏。

t: 面向代码的提示工程 — AI 编程
d: 面向代码生成与 agentic 任务的 prompt engineering:给足上下文、用验收标准规约需求、先要计划、few-shot 示例、测试驱动 prompting、分解大改动,以及常见反模式。

t: AI 时代的 SDE 面试 — AI 编程
d: AI 时代的 SDE 面试还在考什么?样板坍缩后存活下来的技能——系统设计、调试、代码评审、需求澄清、权衡沟通,以及把 AI 当学习放大器而非拐杖。

t: 工具使用、函数调用与 MCP — AI 编程
d: function calling(tool use)与 MCP 深入:三阶段交互、用 JSON Schema 定义工具、tool-use 循环、并行调用、MCP 架构(Host/Client/Server)与三种原语,以及安全考量。

t: 联系与模拟面试
d: 预约面向 SDE 面试的 1:1 模拟面试或咨询。系统设计与编码面试准备。

t: DDIA 笔记 — 《设计数据密集型应用》
d: 《设计数据密集型应用》(DDIA)浓缩笔记——可靠性、数据模型、存储引擎等等。

t: DDIA 第1章 — 可靠、可扩展与可维护
d: 可靠性、可扩展性、可维护性——DDIA 第 1 章讲清故障 vs 失效、负载参数与百分位延迟,以及可运维性、简单性、可演化性三支柱。

t: DDIA 第10章 — 批处理
d: 批处理系统揭秘——DDIA 第 10 章讲 Unix 哲学、MapReduce、join 与倾斜、不可变输入容错,以及 Spark/Flink 数据流引擎。

t: DDIA 第11章 — 流处理
d: 流处理实战——DDIA 第 11 章讲事件流、Kafka 日志代理、CDC、事件溯源、窗口、流/表 join 与 exactly-once。

t: DDIA 第12章 — 数据系统的未来
d: 数据系统的未来——DDIA 第 12 章讲数据集成、解绑数据库、数据流派生、端到端正确性、完整性 vs 及时性,以及数据伦理。

t: DDIA 第2章 — 数据模型与查询语言
d: 关系、文档、图数据模型如何取舍——DDIA 第 2 章讲阻抗失配、读时/写时 schema 与声明式 vs 命令式查询(SQL/Cypher/SPARQL)。

t: DDIA 第3章 — 存储与检索
d: 数据库如何存储与检索——DDIA 第 3 章对比 LSM-tree 与 B-tree、WAL、索引、OLTP vs OLAP 与数据仓库列式存储压缩。

t: DDIA 第4章 — 编码与演化
d: 数据编码与 schema 演化——DDIA 第 4 章讲向后/向前兼容、Protobuf/Thrift/Avro 与数据库、REST/RPC、消息代理数据流。

t: DDIA 第5章 — 复制
d: 单主、多主、无主复制如何取舍——DDIA 第 5 章详解同步 vs 异步、故障转移、复制滞后与读己之写,以及 quorum(w+r>n)。

t: DDIA 第6章 — 分区
d: 数据分区(分片)实战——DDIA 第 6 章讲键范围 vs 哈希分区、热点、本地/全局二级索引、再平衡,以及请求路由。

t: DDIA 第7章 — 事务
d: 事务到底意味着什么——DDIA 第 7 章拆解 ACID、隔离级别、丢失更新与写偏斜,以及可串行化三种实现(串行执行、2PL、SSI)。

t: DDIA 第8章 — 分布式系统的麻烦
d: 分布式系统为何这么难——DDIA 第 8 章讲部分失败、不可靠网络与时钟、进程暂停、fencing token、Byzantine 故障与系统模型。

t: DDIA 第9章 — 一致性与共识
d: 线性一致性与共识——DDIA 第 9 章讲 CAP、因果排序(Lamport/版本向量)、全序广播、2PC 与 Paxos/Raft/ZooKeeper。

t: 系统设计面试题与拆解
d: 系统设计面试准备——电商、视频流、聊天应用、打车、URL 短链等等。

t: 设计聊天应用 — 系统设计
d: 聊天应用（WhatsApp/微信）系统设计：WebSocket 路由、Snowflake ID、离线投递去重、写/读扇出、群聊有序、已读回执、在线状态、媒体存储与取舍。

t: 设计电商平台 — 系统设计
d: 电商平台系统设计：微服务与 API 网关、多语言持久化、结账 Saga、订单状态机、库存防超卖、支付幂等、CDC 搜索、高峰扩展与关键取舍。

t: 设计 Google Drive / Dropbox — 系统设计
d: Google Drive/Dropbox 文件同步系统设计：分块、内容寻址去重、元数据与块分离、同步算法、冲突解决、共享权限、CDN 下载与取舍。

t: 设计酒店预订应用 — 系统设计
d: 酒店预订（Booking/Expedia）系统设计：SQL+ACID、CHECK 约束防超卖、乐观锁、Redis TTL 持有、ES 搜索、状态机、支付幂等与取舍。

t: 设计分布式键值存储 — 系统设计
d: Dynamo 式分布式键值存储系统设计：一致性哈希与虚拟节点、N 路复制、quorum 可调一致性(W+R>N)、向量时钟、Merkle 反熵、gossip 与 AP 取舍。

t: 设计实时排行榜 — 系统设计
d: 实时排行榜系统设计：为何 SQL 不扩展、Redis 有序集合(跳表)、ZADD/ZREVRANK、持久化、top-k 与邻居、分片、精确 vs 近似排名与取舍。

t: 设计地图 Web 应用 — 系统设计
d: 地图平台（Google Maps）系统设计：路网图模型、空间索引(Geohash/Quadtree/S2)、瓦片 CDN、CH 路由、WebSocket 导航、交通融合与取舍。

t: 设计附近的人 / Yelp(邻近服务)— 系统设计
d: 附近的人/Yelp 邻近服务系统设计：地理空间索引、为何 B-tree 失效、Geohash 前缀、Quadtree 自适应、边界 8 邻居、两步读路径与取舍。

t: 设计 Pastebin — 系统设计
d: Pastebin 文本分享系统设计：key 生成(随机 vs 计数器)、元数据与内容分离(DB 指针+S3)、读写路径、缓存 CDN、过期清理与取舍。

t: 设计支付系统 — 系统设计
d: 支付系统设计：正确性优先、幂等键防双重扣款、状态机与 webhook、复式记账账本、exactly-once、Saga、对账、PCI 合规与关键取舍。

t: 设计打车应用 — 系统设计
d: 打车应用（Uber/Lyft）系统设计：GPS 摄取、地理空间索引(Geohash/S2)、内存索引+Cassandra、二分图匹配、行程状态机、ETA、动态定价与取舍。

t: 设计 Ticketmaster / 抢票 — 系统设计
d: Ticketmaster 抢票系统设计：强一致库存防超卖、座位状态机与临时持有、原子并发控制、虚拟候车室削峰、浏览 AP/预订 CP、支付 saga 与取舍。

t: 设计搜索自动补全 (Typeahead) — 系统设计
d: 搜索自动补全（typeahead）系统设计：trie 前缀树、节点预计算 top-k、热度时间衰减排序、离线管道建 trie、按前缀分片、缓存防抖与取舍。

t: 设计一个 URL 短链 (TinyURL) — 系统设计
d: URL 短链（TinyURL）系统设计：容量估算、短 ID 生成(哈希 vs Snowflake)、布隆过滤器、Redis 缓存、301/302、自定义别名、点击分析与取舍。

t: 设计视频流平台 — 系统设计
d: 视频流平台（YouTube/Netflix）系统设计：TCP/UDP/QUIC、Kafka 上传、转码与 HLS/DASH ABR、Blob+Cassandra、CDN 签名 URL、离线推荐与取舍。

t: 技术栈深入 — Kafka、Kubernetes、缓存等
d: 技术栈深入——Kafka、Kubernetes、负载均衡、缓存、分片、NoSQL、OAuth、Spark、GraphQL vs REST。

t: API 网关与服务网格
d: 横切关注点、南北 vs 东西向流量、BFF、sidecar、mTLS,以及网关与网格的区别。

t: 缓存 — Cache-Aside、淘汰策略与 Redis vs Memcached
d: 分层缓存、五种读写策略、淘汰算法、TTL、击穿/穿透/雪崩、热点 key、一致性、Redis vs Memcached、CDN。

t: CDN — 内容分发网络
d: 边缘缓存、PoP 与 anycast、Cache-Control 与 TTL、推与拉、失效与动态加速。

t: CI/CD — 持续集成与交付
d: CI vs 交付 vs 部署、流水线阶段、不可变制品、部署策略(滚动、蓝绿、金丝雀)与回滚。

t: 数据库索引 — B-Tree 对比 LSM-Tree
d: 为什么需要索引、B-Tree 与 LSM-Tree、聚簇与二级索引、复合与覆盖索引、最左前缀规则。

t: DNS — 互联网的名字系统
d: 名字如何解析成 IP、DNS 层级、记录类型、缓存与 TTL、GeoDNS 与 anycast,以及可靠性。

t: Docker 与容器
d: 容器如何用 namespaces 和 cgroups 隔离、容器对比虚拟机、镜像分层、Dockerfile、registry。

t: Elasticsearch — 搜索与分析引擎
d: 倒排索引、分析器、BM25 相关性、分片与副本、scatter-gather、近实时,以及 query 与 filter。

t: GraphQL 与 REST 对比
d: GraphQL vs REST:解析器与 N+1、DataLoader、缓存、schema 设计、分页、订阅、联邦、安全、REST/GraphQL/gRPC 决策。

t: gRPC 与 Protobuf
d: 契约优先 RPC、protobuf 二进制格式、HTTP/2、四种流式调用,以及 gRPC 对比 REST/GraphQL。

t: Apache Kafka — 发布订阅、分区与精确一次
d: 分布式事件流平台:发布/订阅、分区与副本、acks 与 ISR、消费者组、日志压实、KRaft、调优与生态。

t: Kubernetes (k8s) — Pod、Service 与集群架构
d: 容器编排平台:Pod/Service、Master/Worker 架构、调度、网络、存储、探针、自动扩缩与 Operator 模式。

t: 负载均衡 — 算法、L4 vs L7 与健康检查
d: L4 vs L7、负载均衡算法、健康检查、粘性会话、一致性哈希、TLS 终止、DNS/Anycast、高可用与服务网格。

t: 消息队列 — 投递语义、死信队列与 Kafka 对比
d: 队列 vs 提交日志、三种投递保证、有序与分区、推/拉模型、背压、死信队列、RabbitMQ vs SQS vs Kafka。

t: NoSQL — 文档/键值/列族与 CAP 定理
d: 四大类 NoSQL、CAP/PACELC、一致性模型、BASE vs ACID、数据建模、DynamoDB/MongoDB/Cassandra/Neo4j。

t: OAuth 2.0 — 授权码、PKCE 与授权类型
d: OAuth 2.0 授权框架:角色、OIDC、四种授权类型、PKCE、JWT 校验、scope、state/CSRF、令牌防盗。

t: 对象存储与 S3
d: 对象 vs 块 vs 文件、扁平命名空间、分段/预签名上传、纠删码、一致性与分级。

t: 可观测性 — 指标、日志与追踪
d: 监控 vs 可观测性、三大支柱、分布式追踪、OpenTelemetry、SLI/SLO/SLA 与告警。

t: Redis — 内存数据结构存储
d: 为什么单线程却很快、数据类型、RDB 与 AOF 持久化、淘汰、复制与集群,以及各种用途。

t: 分片 — 一致性哈希、分片键与数据分区
d: 水平/垂直分区、三种分片策略、一致性哈希、分片键设计、热点与重分片、跨分片查询/事务、分片 vs 复制。

t: Spark Join 策略
d: Spark join 策略:广播/sort-merge/shuffle hash/笛卡尔、shuffle 成本、数据倾斜、Bucketing、AQE、调优。

t: 向量数据库 — Embedding 与 ANN 搜索
d: embedding、相似度度量、近似最近邻(HNSW/IVF)、RAG 与混合搜索。

t: WebSockets 与实时
d: 轮询 vs 长轮询 vs SSE vs WebSockets、升级握手、全双工,以及用发布/订阅背板扩展。

t: Design ChatGPT — an AI Chat Assistant at Scale
d: ChatGPT system design — stream tokens over SSE, store conversations, scale the inference layer behind an LLM gateway, plus memory, RAG, safety, and cost.

t: Design a RAG System at Scale — System Design
d: RAG system design — chunk and embed documents, build an ANN vector index, run hybrid retrieval and reranking, ground generation, and handle freshness and cost.

t: Evals for LLM Apps — How to Test an Agent
d: Evals for LLM apps: how to test a probabilistic system and an agent — eval datasets, grading methods, LLM-as-judge, trajectory eval, and offline vs online.

t: 设计 ChatGPT —— 大规模 AI 聊天助手
d: ChatGPT 式 AI 聊天助手系统设计：流式响应、会话存储、推理层、LLM 网关、记忆与 RAG、安全与成本，以及面试取舍。

t: 设计大规模 RAG 系统 —— 系统设计
d: 大规模 RAG（检索增强生成）系统设计：摄入与分块、向量化、向量索引(ANN)、混合检索、重排、有据生成、新鲜度、评测、成本与取舍。

t: LLM 应用的评测 —— 如何测试一个 Agent
d: LLM 应用的评测:如何真正测试一个概率系统与 Agent——评测数据集、打分方法(启发式、代码、LLM-as-judge、人工)、Agent 轨迹评测、离线门禁 vs 在线评测,以及那些坑。

t: Loop Engineering — Designing the Agent Loop
d: Loop engineering is designing the agent loop itself: the plan-act-observe cycle, stopping conditions, step and cost budgets, in-loop verification, and recovery.

t: Loop Engineering 循环工程 —— 设计 Agent 循环
d: Loop Engineering(循环工程):设计 Agent 循环本身——plan-act-observe、停止条件、步数/成本预算、循环内验证、错误恢复与死循环检测、循环拓扑,以及如何用评测来调。

t: Design a News Feed (Twitter) — System Design
d: News feed system design (Twitter/Facebook timeline) — fan-out-on-write vs fan-out-on-read, celebrity hot-key problem, hybrid fan-out, and Redis timeline cache.

t: 设计 News Feed (Twitter 时间线) — 系统设计
d: News Feed（Twitter/Facebook 时间线）系统设计：写时扇出 vs 读时扇出、名人热 key 问题、混合扇出、Redis 时间线缓存、Feed 排序与分页取舍。

t: Design a Notification System — System Design
d: Notification system design — multi-channel push/SMS/email/in-app, APNs & FCM, fan-out, dedup, rate limiting, retries, DLQ, at-least-once delivery guarantees.

t: 设计一个通知系统 — 系统设计
d: 通知系统设计:多渠道 push/SMS/email/in-app、APNs 与 FCM、扇出、去重、限流、重试与 DLQ、用户偏好、模板服务、可观测性与 at-least-once 投递保证。

t: Design a Web Crawler — System Design
d: Web crawler system design: URL frontier with priority and politeness queues, DNS caching, robots.txt, Bloom-filter dedup, crawl traps, and recrawl freshness.

t: 设计一个网络爬虫 — 系统设计
d: 网络爬虫系统设计:URL frontier 的优先级与礼貌性队列、DNS 缓存、robots.txt、布隆过滤器去重、爬虫陷阱、分布式 worker 与新鲜度重爬。

t: Design a Rate Limiter — System Design
d: Rate limiter design: token bucket vs leaky bucket vs sliding window, distributed Redis counters, Lua atomicity, 429 responses, where it sits at the API gateway.

t: 设计一个限流器 (Rate Limiter) — 系统设计
d: 限流器系统设计：token bucket vs leaky bucket vs 滑动窗口、Redis 分布式计数器、Lua 原子性、429 响应、限流器放在 API 网关。算法、原子性与取舍。
